title: How artificial intelligence got better at building itself
text:
Over and over 


How artificial intelligence got better at building itself 

WHEN ANTHROPIC, an artificial-intelligence lab, debuts on stock markets later this year, it is likely to be one of the biggest initial public offerings in history. That’s because Claude, the company’s chatbot is beloved of coders, who are willing to pay a lot for access. Since Claude Code, its software-engineering agent, launched in February 2025, it has become indispensable for developers around the world. That includes Anthropic’s own: more than four-fifths of the code it published in May was written by Claude, the company says. Before Claude Code, the percentage was “low single-digits”. 

The systems have improved in quality of output as well as quantity. An influential benchmark from METR, a think-tank, shows that in early 2025 Anthropic’s models could complete tasks that took human engineers a little under an hour. The company’s latest systems can complete tasks that would take more than a working day. 

And so it may be easy to raise a cynical eyebrow when the company, at the top of its game and outclassing the competition, calls for the world to have “the option to slow or temporarily pause frontier AI development”, as it did on June 5th. What market leader would not wish that its competition stop trying to catch up?

quizlet: at the top of its game
at the top of its game
more than four-fifths of the code
quality of output as well as quantity

questions.question: Why is Anthropic expected to have one of the largest IPOs in history?
options: Because Claude has become highly valuable and popular
Because it owns several stock exchanges
questions.explanation: The article says Claude is widely used and people are willing to pay for access.

questions.question: How much of Anthropic's code was reportedly written by Claude in May?
questions.explanation: The company states that more than four-fifths of its code was written by Claude.

questions.question: What does the METR benchmark show?
options: Anthropic's models can complete increasingly complex tasks
questions.explanation: The benchmark shows that newer models can complete tasks that once required much more human time.

questions.question: Why might some people be surprised by Anthropic's call to slow AI development?
options: The company is leaving the AI industry
The company currently leads the market
The company does not use AI itself
questions.explanation: As a market leader, Anthropic benefits from its competitive advantage.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'trying to catch up' refer to?
options: Competitors attempting to reach Anthropic's level
questions.explanation: The phrase refers to rival companies trying to match Anthropic's progress.
